review_scope: nuclear pore complexes and gate cores on the approved Phase 02R envelope

score_evidence.notes: Score is manual and scoped to pore readability, not automated similarity. The builder first produced pores that were too bulky, then reduced protrusion and reframed the close-up before this review.

passed_checks: Reference image and pore-bearing candidate are visible in the same Blender scene.
Pore complexes are raised, embedded into the envelope, and visibly distributed on the outer membrane.
Pore openings include gate-core filaments so the model does not teach pores as passive open holes.
Pore rings are custom generated meshes rather than default torus primitives.
Lamina, nucleoplasm, chromatin, nucleolus, cargo, ER connection, and mitosis artifacts were not added in this phase.
minor_issues: Some pore rings still read slightly regular from the board distance; later polishing should add more irregular protein subunit variation.
The pore density is appropriate for this review slice but not yet enough for a final whole-nucleus hero model.
The close-up camera now proves the gate/opening, but a future review should add one more exterior grazing angle after the lamina is added.
required_fixes_before_final_export: Create a Roblox LOD plan for pore rings and gate filaments.
Bake or simplify small gate filaments for lower-end Roblox devices.
Do not add transport cargo until the pore/gate animation contract exists.
decision_notes: Phase 02P passes as a scoped pore-complex foundation. It authorizes the next gated build scope: nuclear lamina only. It does not approve nucleoplasm, chromatin, nucleolus, transport cargo, rough ER, or mitosis fragments.
